metadata.description: Research intelligence topics for Changeis Inc. Drives automated monitoring, daily briefs, and opportunity tracking.

changes: Updated T01 (FAA ATEPS-II): Added ASRC Federal $437M SLES award (Apr 7, 2026) — competitive FAA NAS engineering services vehicle to monitor for future ATEPS-adjacent task orders; added context on FAA Peraton BNATCS $1.5B contract (prime integrator) and $19-20B supplemental request from Sec. Duffy; noted HigherGov listing shows SAIC ATEPS-II Screening Information Request (SIR-7) posted with Apr 16 2026 deadline — early indicator of upcoming task order competition

Updated T02 (FAA NAS Modernization): Brand New Air Traffic Control System (BNATCS) — Peraton confirmed as $1.5B prime integrator (contract value disclosed Feb 10 2026); radar replacement installations beginning Q2 2026 (612 radars by Jun 2028, $1.1B RTX/Indra); CAP (Common Automation Platform) next-gen ATC system under development; DOT Secretary Duffy seeking additional $19-20B for full overhaul

Updated T03 (DHS CISA Recompetes): CISA IDIQ RFP now expected mid-2026 (slipped from Feb 2026 projection); added new recompete: CISA Enterprise Engineering & Operations Support Services (CEEOSS) — Sev1Tech incumbent, GSA Schedule, award Q3 FY2026, sol. released Feb 2026; DEFEND task order extensions confirmed through April 30, 2027

Updated T04 (DHS Component IT): No new material changes week of Apr 14-20; CBP C4I solicitation released Mar 25 per schedule; CBP Tech Service Desk sol. tracking for ~May 2026; added note on ICE SEVP IT/PMO (6 bidders, $35.3M) as lower-competition opportunity

Updated T06 (Federal AI Policy): GSA GSAR 552.239-7001 comment period closed Apr 3 2026 — awaiting final rule for Refresh 32; White House National AI Framework (Mar 20 2026) companion legislative recommendations released; EO signed Mar 26 2026 on DEI requiring new clause in all contracts by Apr 25 2026 (tangential compliance impact); Bloomberg Gov reports FY2026 federal consulting market at $18.7B — rebounding from DOGE cuts

Updated T07 (DOGE Impact): Bloomberg Government data (Apr 13 2026): FY2026 civilian professional services market at $18.7B, within 0.5% of FY2025 levels — market rebounding; DOGE-related 8(a) contract reviews (Wave 3, Jan 2026) compliance audits completed Feb 28 2026 — monitoring for enforcement actions; NDAA Jun 2026 cost/pricing data threshold rising from $2.5M to $10M; total estimated DOGE savings claimed: $215B (disputed)

Updated T08 (Federal Cloud/Zero Trust): NEW: CISA issued binding operational directive requiring all federal civilian agencies to implement zero trust architecture by Sep 30, 2026 — accelerated from prior timeline by 6 months in response to government cyberattacks; NSA Zero Trust Implementation Guideline Primer (Jan 2026) formalizes 5-phase ZT framework; DoD ZT FY2027 deadline unchanged

Updated T10 (GSA OASIS+/Contract Vehicles): NEW: GSA Alliant 3 GWAC Phase 1 awards issued Feb 20 2026 (43 awardees including Booz Allen, SAIC, Peraton, ECS, CACI, Leidos, Deloitte, GDIT, BAE Systems, Accenture, ManTech, Serco, Empower AI, Sev1Tech); Notice to Proceed issued Mar 10 2026 — Alliant 3 is now live for ordering through Mar 2031; OASIS+ Phase 2 continuous enrollment remains open (no close date); FAQ doc updated Apr 15 2026; SMS auth for Symphony Portal ending Apr 30 2026

Updated T11 (CMMC 2.0): Corrected Phase 2 hard deadline — Nov 10 2026 (not Oct 31 2026) is when mandatory Level 2 C3PAO certification becomes required for applicable new contracts; Oct 31 2026 figure was widely circulated but Nov 10 is the accurate 48 CFR rule anniversary; DoD FY2027 ZT deadline aligns with Phase 3

Updated T17 (Army MAPS): Amendment 01 released April 14 2026 — KEY CHANGES: (1) QP recency window anchored to May 1 2026 — only projects with performance completed on/after May 1 2023 qualify (firms must re-check QPs); (2) Passthrough rate scoring now applies to both prime and subcontractor QPs; (3) CUI supporting documents now require separate submission via DoD Safe (not CHESS); (4) Cover letter updated with new CUI identification section. Proposals due May 1 2026 still — NO EXTENSION. Last updated Apr 16 on SAM.gov.

Updated T14 (Competitive Intel): Alliant 3 Phase 1 awardees confirmed — Booz Allen, SAIC, Peraton, ECS Federal, CACI, Deloitte, GDIT, BAE Systems, Accenture Federal, Leidos, ManTech, Serco, Empower AI, Sev1Tech, Agile Defense, Guidehouse, KBR Wyle, others (43 total). Booz Allen projecting low-double-digit civilian revenue contraction FY2026 — teaming window open. DOGE Wave 3 (Jan 2026) 8(a) pass-through reviews could create incumbent vulnerability on small-business vehicles.
Updated metadata: version 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0, last_reviewed updated to 2026-04-20, next_review updated to 2026-04-27
Updated T03 (DHS CISA Recompetes): Added $18-20B CISA IDIQ successor vehicle details; updated PDNS value from $128.5M to $105M (corrected); added CDM-DEFEND successor contract tracking

Updated T04 (DHS Enterprise IT): Expanded to include CBP C5I C4I recompete ($50-100M), CBP Cyber Security Operations Support recompete ($100M+), ICE ITSS recompete ($201M), ICE Enterprise Support Services ($63.9M)
Added T15 (TSA IT and Operations): New critical topic for TSA NEXUS ($100M+), TEAS ($50-100M), FOCUS ($100M+) contracts all due Q2-Q3 FY2026
Added T16 (USCG Cybersecurity and C5I): New high priority topic for Coast Guard Cyber Command IOM contract and CG-C5I sustainment opportunities

Updated T07 (DOGE Impact): Refined notes — DOGE confirmed sunset July 4, 2026; 10,000+ contracts terminated; fixed-price and cost-justification requirements persist; IT consulting >$10M requires DOGE approval still
Updated T08 (Federal Cloud/Zero Trust): Added CISA IDIQ as Zero Trust delivery vehicle
Updated T10 (GSA MAS): OASIS legacy ended; OASIS+ Phase 2 open enrollment as of Jan 2026 — no formal deadline; updated as key vehicle
Updated T13 (FAR Overhaul/RFO): Corrected framing — FAR Part 12 commercial-first, not Part 10; added CSO and OTA references
Updated T14 (Competitive Intel): Added Synergy as USCG incumbent; noted CACI as CBP C4I incumbent; Leidos as CBP Cyber incumbent
Updated Army MAPS: T17 added — RFP officially issued April 6, 2026 (confirmed live); offers due May 1, 2026; awards September 2026
Updated T06 (Federal AI Policy): Added White House National AI Framework (March 20, 2026); OPM AI Workforce programs; TRUMP AMERICA AI Act
Removed T11 (CMMC 2.0): Lowered priority — frequency changed to monthly, notes updated with Oct 31 2026 hard gate; retained but de-prioritized
Updated metadata: version 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0, next_review updated

topics.name: FAA ATEPS-II Task Orders
topics.notes: IDIQ ceiling: Noblis $709.1M (awarded Dec 2024), SAIC $629.8M (awarded Jul 2025). Task order competition is active battleground through 2035. SETIS contract also active for additional FAA engineering task orders. NEW (Apr 2026): SAIC ATEPS-II Screening Information Request (SIR-7) posted with Apr 16, 2026 deadline — early indicator of upcoming task order competition; monitor for formal RFP. NEW: ASRC Federal awarded $437M Second Level Engineering Services (SLES) contract Apr 7, 2026 — 5-year IDIQ for 24/7 field support, NAS maintenance, systems integration. Multiple eFAST recompetes also active in 2026 ($33M NextGen Initiative TO, $128.4M I&O Services, others). Primary teaming opportunity for Changeis.

topics.name: FAA NAS Modernization
topics.notes: Broader FAA air traffic organization spend beyond ATEPS-II. MAJOR UPDATE (Feb 2026): Peraton selected as $1.5B prime integrator for Brand New Air Traffic Control System (BNATCS) — $12.5B total program. Contract disclosed Feb 10 2026 by FAA Administrator Bedford; 40% of telecom connections upgraded to fiber/wireless as of Dec 2025; 612 radar replacements by Jun 2028 ($1.1B, RTX/Indra). DOT Sec. Duffy seeking additional $19-20B supplemental funding. CAP (Common Automation Platform) — next-gen single ATC system handling both civil and military — in development. Peraton subcontracting pipeline significant — monitor for teaming opportunities. Emerging areas: UAM/AAM integration, UAS UTM, autonomous flight — all generating new SETIS task orders.

topics.name: DHS CISA Recompetes and New IDIQ Vehicle
topics.notes: Three parallel tracks: (1) ~24 FY2026 CISA task order recompetes including $321M DEFEND F (7 bidders), $271M Program Mgmt Support (3 bidders), $105M PDNS (6 bidders), $20M Integrated Ops Div, $12.6M PERT Support. (2) Major new vehicle: $18-20B CISA IDIQ (GSA-assisted), covering 5 service areas + products, replaces CDM DEFEND structure; RFP originally projected Feb 2026 — now slipped to mid-2026 (Q2-Q3), award Feb 2027. All DEFEND task orders extended to April 30, 2027. (3) NEW (Apr 2026): CISA Enterprise Engineering & Operations Support Services (CEEOSS) — Sev1Tech incumbent, GSA Schedule, Labor Hour, award Q3 FY2026, solicitation released Feb 2026 — covers modern IT infrastructure, emerging technologies, hybrid cloud, mobility/collaboration for CISA OCIO. Small business set-asides expected in SA5 and PA1 of new IDIQ. High BD priority — pursue teaming NOW.

topics.name: DHS Component IT Recompetes — CBP, ICE, FLETC
topics.notes: Active recompetes: CBP C4I Systems (CACI incumbent, $50-100M, Alliant 2, award Q4 FY2026, solicitation released Mar 25, 2026 per schedule); CBP Cyber Security Ops Support (Leidos incumbent, $100M+, award Q2-Q3 FY2026); CBP Tech Service Desk (Patriot LLC incumbent, $100M+, STARS III/8a, sol. ~May 2026). ICE recompetes: $201M ITSS (12 bidders), $63.9M Enterprise Support Services (14 bidders), $35.3M SEVP IT/PMO (6 bidders — lower competition opportunity), $10.1M Internet Research & Data Analytics (3 bidders — lowest competition). UPDATE (Apr 20 2026): No material status changes week of Apr 14-20; CBP C4I on track per Mar 25 sol. release. $191B+ DHS funding from One Big Beautiful Bill creates expanded pipeline.

topics.name: FMCSA Motus System Support
topics.notes: Phase 1 (supporting companies) live December 2025 at motus.dot.gov. Phase 2 (all users: carriers, brokers, freight forwarders) rolling out through 2026. Monitor for IT support, integration, and enhancement opportunities as platform scales. Proposed rulemaking estimated March 2026. This is a multi-year O&M and enhancement opportunity.

topics.name: Federal AI Policy, Governance, and Workforce
topics.notes: White House released National Policy Framework for AI on March 20, 2026 — nonbinding but shapes federal AI legislation; companion legislative recommendations released same day. EO 14365 (Dec 2025) directing review of state AI laws; DOJ AI Litigation Task Force established Jan 9 2026. OMB M-25-21 and M-25-22 (Apr 3 2025) — primary AI governance and acquisition memos for agencies. GSA GSAR 552.239-7001 comment period closed April 3, 2026 — GSA deferred inclusion in MAS Refresh 31; targeting Refresh 32 (spring/summer 2026) — MONITOR for final rule that will impose 'American AI Systems' mandate, 72-hour incident reporting, data rights provisions, and 'Unbiased AI Principles'. NEW (Mar 26 2026): EO on DEI discrimination by federal contractors — new clause required in all contracts by Apr 25, 2026. OPM: US Tech Force (1,000 AI/cyber fellows/year), Data Science Fellows (250, Spring 2026). Cleared AI professional talent gap acute. Changeis staffing + AI delivery positioning is primary opportunity.

topics.name: DOGE Contracting Impact and Post-DOGE Landscape
topics.notes: DOGE sunsets July 4, 2026 (functions absorbing into OMB/OPM permanently). Over 10,000 contracts terminated/descoped; 273 contracts ($5.1B ceiling) terminated in 4-week window (Feb 2026). IT consulting contracts >$10M still require DOGE approval; advisory services >$1M require approval. Fixed-price preference and public cost-justification requirements persist post-DOGE. MARKET REBOUND (Apr 13 2026): Bloomberg Government reports FY2026 federal civilian consulting market at $18.7B — within 0.5% of FY2025 levels — first growth signal since DOGE began. DoD absorbed $11.1B in DOGE efficiency cuts (Navy $3.7B, Army $3.2B, AF $2.3B). Wave 3 (Jan 2026): All DoD 8(a) contracts >$20M subject to pass-through compliance audit — initial assessment due Jan 31, audit due Feb 28 — monitoring for enforcement actions/re-solicitations. NDAA Jun 2026: cost/pricing data threshold rising from $2.5M to $10M. Monitor for re-solicitation of terminated contracts — DOGE-terminated contracts creating new short-fuse BD opportunities.

topics.name: Federal Cloud Migration and Zero Trust
topics.notes: CRITICAL UPDATE (Apr 17 2026): CISA issued binding operational directive requiring ALL federal civilian agencies to implement zero trust architecture by September 30, 2026 — timeline accelerated by 6 months from prior deadline in response to surge of cyberattacks on government systems; $2.1B in emergency funding allocated. NSA Zero Trust Implementation Guideline Primer (Jan 2026) formalizes 5-phase ZT framework for reaching target. DoD FY2027 Zero Trust deadline unchanged — all components, defense agencies, and DIB must achieve target-level ZT by end of FY2027. JWCC Next active. Air Force Cloud One $88M Oracle task order Feb 2026. New CISA $18-20B IDIQ includes zero trust and CDM capabilities as core service areas. FCEB agencies under intense pressure to meet Sep 30 2026 ZT milestone — expect surge in ZT implementation task orders Q2-Q3 FY2026.

topics.name: Government AI/ML Implementation
topics.notes: Agencies transitioning AI pilots to full production through 2026. CFO Act agencies required to publish AI strategies by Oct 2025 (done) and update acquisition policies by Dec 29, 2025. OPM US Tech Force (1,000 fellows/year) and Data Science Fellows program (250, Spring 2026) creating AI demand at agencies. Cleared AI professional talent gap is acute — Changeis staffing + delivery positioning opportunity. White House AI Framework (March 2026) sets nonbinding governance posture; EO 14365 coordinates federal AI policy. GSA GSAR 552.239-7001 'American AI Systems' mandate pending in Refresh 32 — will reshape AI procurement across all GSA schedules.

topics.name: GSA OASIS+ and Contract Vehicles
topics.notes: OASIS (legacy) fully expired — ordering ended Dec 2024 (SB/8a) and Mar 2025 (Unrestricted). OASIS+ Phase 2 continuous open enrollment — all 6 solicitations (SB, 8(a), HUBZone, SDVOSB, WOSB, Unrestricted) open as of Jan 12, 2026 with NO closing date; 13 total domains (5 new: Business Admin, Financial Services, Human Capital, Marketing/PR, Social Services); proposals evaluated on rolling basis in order received. NOTE: SMS authentication for Symphony Portal ending Apr 30, 2026 — must switch to authenticator app. OASIS+ FAQ updated Apr 15 2026. NEW MAJOR: GSA Alliant 3 GWAC — Phase 1 awards announced Feb 20 2026 (43 awardees); Notice to Proceed issued Mar 10 2026 — NOW LIVE for ordering (ordering period Mar 10 2026 to Mar 9 2031 + option); unrestricted GWAC for complex IT services (cloud, cyber, data, software dev, systems engineering, emerging tech); supports EO 14240 procurement consolidation. Changeis should pursue OASIS+ on-ramp if not already on vehicle. STARS III active for 8(a) set-aside CBP task orders.

topics.name: CMMC 2.0 Compliance and DoD Cybersecurity
topics.notes: CMMC Level 1/2 self-attestations now in DoD solicitations since Phase 1 (Nov 10, 2025). CORRECTED DEADLINE (Apr 2026): Phase 2 hard gate is November 10, 2026 (not Oct 31 2026 as previously noted) — this is when mandatory Level 2 C3PAO third-party certification becomes required for applicable new contracts. Phase 3 (Nov 10 2027): L2 required for option exercises; L3 DIBCAC requirements introduced. Phase 4 (Nov 10 2028): Full implementation. 8(a) set-aside DoD contracts >$20M subject to pass-through compliance audit as of Jan 2026 (Wave 3 memo). C3PAO assessment backlog is real — Changeis should confirm compliance posture and schedule assessment NOW to beat queue before Nov 2026 deadline.

topics.name: SAM.gov Opportunity Monitoring — Core NAICS
topics.notes: Broad daily sweep across Changeis core NAICS. Feeds VM2 daily brief. Separate from named-program monitoring. Priority filter: DHS, DOT, Army, GSA agencies. DOGE contract re-solicitations may create short-fuse opportunities — flag any newly released solicitations in terminated contract categories. Bloomberg Gov (Apr 2026): civilian consulting market rebounding to FY2025 levels — increased solicitation volume expected Q2-Q3 FY2026.

topics.name: Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) and Acquisition Reform
topics.notes: EO 14265 targets defense acquisition modernization. FAR Part 12 commercial-first mandate — agencies must prove commercial solution cannot meet need before custom dev. CSO and OTA pathways expanding. Changes Changeis proposal strategy: lead with commercial-off-the-shelf solutions first, custom second. Fixed-price contracting preference reinforced. NDAA Jun 2026: cost/pricing data certified cost threshold rising from $2.5M to $10M — reduces reporting burden on mid-size contracts. Draft DFARS revisions expected 2026 per EO 14265 mandate.

topics.name: Competitive Intelligence — Large Integrators and Incumbents
topics.notes: Key incumbents to track: Noblis (FAA ATEPS-II $709M), SAIC (FAA ATEPS-II $629M), CACI (CBP C4I C5I, CBP ITSS; also $287M Army IPPS-A; Alliant 3 Phase 1 awardee), Leidos (CBP Cyber Security Ops $100M+; Alliant 3 Phase 1 awardee), Synergy (USCG CG-C5I $32M task order Mar 2026), Booz Allen (2x CDM DEFEND groups; Alliant 3 Phase 1 awardee — projecting low-double-digit civilian revenue contraction FY2026), Sev1Tech (CISA CEEOSS incumbent). NEW (Feb 2026): Alliant 3 Phase 1 awardees confirmed (43 companies including all major primes) — these firms now hold a key new GWAC; subcontracting under Alliant 3 is a potential Changeis pathway. DOGE Wave 3 (Jan 2026) 8(a) pass-through audits may create incumbent vulnerability. Monitor for subcontracting/teaming solicitations from all incumbents above.

topics.name: TSA IT Contracts — NEXUS, TEAS, FOCUS
topics.notes: Three active TSA IT procurements all targeting Q2-Q3 FY2026 award: (1) NEXUS — IT Ops & Maintenance, >$100M, FFP BPA, performance through May 2031, Springfield VA — broad O&M scope including cloud, AI, data analytics, telecom; (2) TEAS — Enterprise Engineering & Architecture, $50-100M, FFP BPA, award Q2 FY2026, May 2031 completion — new tech integration and design; (3) FOCUS — Field Ops & Customer Support, >$100M, FFP task order, award Q3 FY2026, SPOC helpdesk + field IT specialist support for ~85,000 TSA personnel at ~500 sites; solicitation released Jan 19 2026 per APFS; not set aside for SB. All new to Changeis BD pipeline — high priority given DHS focus area. Monitor SAM.gov for formal solicitation releases on NEXUS and TEAS.

topics.name: USCG Cybersecurity and C5I Digital Transformation
topics.notes: Active opportunity: USCG Cyber Command Integrated Operations Management (IOM) — published March 2026 on DHS APFS, covers CG-CYBER cyberspace operations, NIPRNET/SIPRNET, analytical and technical SME support. Recent award: Synergy $32M task order for CG-C5I sustainment (Mar 2026) — Synergy is likely incumbent to track for teaming. Also monitor for Arctic Security Cutter IT support contracts (11 ASCs under construction, awarded Feb 2026). CISA ZT BOD acceleration (Apr 2026) may generate USCG-specific ZT implementation task orders under IOM or successor vehicles.

topics.name: Army MAPS — $50B IDIQ
topics.notes: RFP officially issued April 6, 2026 on SAM.gov. Offers due May 1, 2026 — NO EXTENSION. $50B IDIQ, 10-year PoP (5-year base + 5-year option). Consolidates RS3 and ITES-3S vehicles. 350 total awards (70 per domain): 5 domains — Engineering/Logistics/Ops, Management/Advisory, RDT&E, Emerging IT, Foundational IT. 30 large business + 25 SB + 15 Commercial-Sector Vendor per domain. AMENDMENT 01 RELEASED APRIL 14, 2026 — KEY CHANGES: (1) QP recency window anchored to May 1, 2026 — only projects completed on/after May 1, 2023 qualify (firms MUST re-check all QPs immediately); (2) Passthrough rate scoring now applies to both prime AND subcontractor QPs; (3) CUI supporting documents must be submitted separately via DoD Safe (NOT CHESS); (4) Cover letter updated with CUI identification section requirement. SAM.gov last updated Apr 16. Gate criteria: Secret facility clearance, ISO 9001:2015, CMMC Level 2 (self), accounting system (LB), CPARS ≤5% marginal. Awards September 2026. CRITICAL ACTION: Proposals due May 1 — review Amendment 01 changes immediately, re-verify QP dates, update CUI submission plan.
topics.action_required: URGENT: Offers due May 1, 2026. Amendment 01 (Apr 14) changed QP recency rules and CUI submission requirements — review immediately and update proposal accordingly.
